refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall analyze the worksite area by identifying terrain features, detecting obstacles, and representing the work-zone accurately.
refined_fr.rationale: Breaking down the broad worksite mapping requirement into specific sub-functions ensures clarity and targeted verification. Terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation are distinct aspects critical for operational safety and planning.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify through sensor data validation for terrain and obstacle detection, and confirm the generated work-zone model matches site plans via simulation or field inspection.
sub_frs: Analyze terrain features of the worksite area
Detect obstacles within the worksite area
Represent the work-zone accurately in the worksite model

candidate_dps: Use LIDAR and camera sensor fusion for terrain and obstacle analysis
Apply real-time data processing algorithms for feature extraction
Generate digital work-zone maps with geospatial tagging
selected_dp.selected_dp: Use LIDAR and camera sensor fusion for terrain and obstacle analysis

potential_sources: Data dependency between terrain and obstacle detection algorithms
Integration of sensor fusion outputs into work-zone representation
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically separates sensing and processing but requires well-defined data interfaces to avoid tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of high-fidelity sensors and reliable data fusion algorithms
Risk of sensor data inaccuracies affecting model quality
High safety criticality demands robust error handling and fallback modes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing sensor data processing to isolate fault domains and improve maintainability.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for integrating sensor data, processing, and generating the worksite model

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives raw sensor data streams (LIDAR, camera) for processing

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends processed worksite model data to planning and execution modules

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data exchange between NGHE Solution and remote modules such as Teleoperation

module_containment_scores.notes: Primary module for worksite mapping; high containment of related functions with minimal diffusion.
Limited containment; vehicle provides sensor hardware but processing is external.
Minimal containment; mainly consumes processed data.
Receives safety alerts but does not contain mapping logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing worksite mapping functions within NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and supports safety-critical integration.
containment_risks: Potential overload of NGHE Solution module leading to complexity
Interface dependencies with Vehicle sensors and Teleoperation may introduce latency
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting sensor data acquisition (Vehicle) and processing (NGHE Solution) more explicitly to reduce module complexity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well-defined and the selected DP logically addresses the requirements with clear verification paths.

diffusion_risks: High integration within NGHE Solution may increase module complexity
Interfaces with Vehicle and Teleoperation require robust data synchronization
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as the primary module; diffusion is low but should be monitored.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via sensor data validation and model accuracy checks within NGHE Solution boundaries.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to NGHE Solution module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Consider modularizing sensor data acquisition separately from processing to reduce complexity.
Define interface protocols explicitly to mitigate latency and synchronization risks.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, module complexity and interface risks suggest revising the IM layer to better separate sensor acquisition and processing.
next_level_sub_frs: Analyze terrain features of the worksite area
Detect obstacles within the worksite area
Represent the work-zone accurately in the worksite model

revise.why: To reduce module complexity and improve containment by separating sensor data acquisition (Vehicle) from processing (NGHE Solution).

assumptions: High-fidelity sensors are available and reliable
Data fusion algorithms can be modularized
Interfaces can support required data rates and latency
open_risks: Module complexity leading to maintenance challenges
evidence_needed: Performance benchmarks of sensor fusion algorithms
Latency measurements on Network Link and Telemetry interfaces
Verification results of worksite model accuracy
